name: Improper Handling of Inconsistent Structural Elements
description: The product does not properly handle cases where two or more structural elements that should be consistent contain conflicting or mismatched values. A canonical example is Heartbleed (CVE-2014-0160), where an inconsistent length parameter enabled an out-of-bounds read exposing cryptographic keys and sensitive data.
detection_pattern: Look for INGRESS nodes (protocol messages, structured data with multiple related fields) where structural metadata fields (e.g., length, count, offset) flow to TRANSFORM nodes (buffer operations, memory access, deserialization) without a CONTROL mediator (consistency check, cross-field validation) that verifies the structural elements agree with each other and with actual data boundaries.
